name: Batter-Dipped Fondue Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: You can omit the butter and increase the salad oil to 2 1/2 cups.
~-------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Mix the ground chuck, egg, bread crumbs, beer or apple juice, and garlic salt in a large bowl until combined.
2. Shape the mixture into 3/4-inch balls and place them on a plate.
3. Prepare the frothy batter by mixing the Bisquick baking mix, beer or apple juice, and egg in another bowl until slightly lumpy.
4. Heat the salad oil in a metal fondue pot to 375 degrees F (190°C).
5. Using a fondue fork, carefully dip each meatball into the hot oil for about 2 minutes, or until cooked through and golden brown.
6. Serve the meatballs with both Mustard Sauce and Hors d'oeuvre Sauce on the side.
NOTE: These meatballs can also be cooked without the batter by simply frying them in the hot oil until cooked through.
